citations.title: Meta Is Turning AI Glasses Into A Subscription
Smart Glasses 101: I've Tried Every Type—Here's What They Actually Do
Meta debuts new, cheaper smart glasses under its own brand
Google Gemini Can Now Control Your Computer. Hackers Are Already Targeting AI Agents
Introducing the Gemini 2.5 Computer Use model

design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from title+kind+audience+generated_at → selected architecture = workflow-command-center. AIDA Check: Attention (hands‑free Meta Display hero), Interest (market context + proof), Desire (workflow + media), Action (CTA band). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (brand anchor). Hero Math Verification: clamp headline spans 2–3 lines at 1120px max-width. Bento Density Verification: compare and bento grids use grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: black primary CTA, white secondary CTA, contrast verified.
headline: Meta Display AI assistant for smart glasses that actually gets work done

site_brief.audience: people evaluating AI assistants for Meta smart glasses and display-style wearable workflows
site_brief.goal: Create an indexable SEO use-case page in the /use-cases/ cluster. Target the practical query 'Meta Display AI assistant', show the exact job-to-be-done, and explain how Super can perform the workflow with real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache. Include sibling use-case links so the cluster feels intentional, crawlable, and useful. Add a long-form operator playbook with how-to steps, setup checklist, realistic limits, confirmation moments, and examples that a reader can act on immediately.
site_brief.title: Meta Display AI assistant
site_brief.use_case_intent: show how Super can become the action layer for hands-free Meta Display workflows beyond simple voice answers

summary: A practical, field‑tested guide to using Super as the action layer behind Meta Display and smart‑glasses workflows—going beyond voice answers to real computer use, durable automation, and a reusable computer-use cache.
